title: Data loading package missing from archive — cannot verify CSV loading from data/kaggle/
evidence: cmd/brsoccer-mcp/main.go:8-9 imports brsoccer/internal/data; main.go:16 calls data.Load(*dataDir). All source files reference data.DB, data.Match, data.Player, data.TeamMatches, data.NormalizeTeam — but internal/data/ directory does not exist in archive. defect_rate=1.0 from retort.db confirms build succeeded during original run.
suggestion: Ensure internal/data package is included in archived workspace; the data loading logic and CSV parsing cannot be verified without it

title: Archived workspace missing internal/data package — code cannot compile from archive
evidence: internal/ contains only mcp/ and query/ directories; no data/ directory. All 10 Go files import brsoccer/internal/data (types: DB, Match, Player; functions: TeamMatches, NormalizeTeam, Load). go.sum also missing. Archive cannot reproduce build.
suggestion: Archive process should preserve all source packages including internal/data/ and go.sum

title: Test code coverage at 33.3%
evidence: test_coverage=0.33325 from retort.db; 11 test functions across server_test.go (4) and query_test.go (7) exercise core query and MCP protocol logic but data loading/parsing path is untested in visible code
suggestion: Add tests for CSV parsing, date format handling, team name normalization edge cases

title: go.mod specifies future Go version 1.25.4
evidence: go.mod line 3: 'go 1.25.4'; current stable Go is 1.24.x
suggestion: Use a released Go version in go.mod for reproducibility
